question: What will be the highest overnight number of Ukrainian drones that Russia's Defense Ministry claims to have destroyed on a single night between July 12 and July 24, 2026?

factors.description: Floor already set: two eligible nights (mornings Jul12=349, Jul13=342) already reported; answer >=349 with ~99.6% probability
Intense/escalating regime: 300+ overnight waves near-nightly in July 2026; max of ~11 active nights alone reaches ~430-450
Mass-wave nights (>=460, up to record 660) occur ~10-13% of nights; over 11 nights ~65-75% chance of at least one, pushing max to 500-660+
Possible regime cooling / no event-timed mass strike keeps future nights moderate, capping max near ~410-460

summary: Max overnight Russian MoD claim of Ukrainian drones destroyed across 13 eligible nights (mornings Jul12–24, 2026). Two eligible nights are already reported (Jul12=349, Jul13=342), setting a hard floor of 349 that is exceeded with ~99.6% probability. Forecasting the max over 11 remaining unknown nights (mornings Jul14–24) via Monte Carlo mixture of nightly overnight figures grounded in July 2026 empirical data: lull nights ~35% (70–200), active nights ~52% (center ~360), mass-wave nights ~10–13% (center ~540, record 660 on Jun26). Over 11 nights, ~65–75% chance of at least one mass wave (≥460), pushing the median to ~520. Right-skewed; ~12% chance of a new record (>660), ~1% above 800 (open upper bound). Result robust across mass-wave-frequency scenarios (median 480–590).
reasoning:
My forecast is submitted.

**Summary:** The question asks for the highest single-night overnight Russian MoD drone-interception claim across nights ending July 12–24, 2026.

**Key insight:** Two eligible nights were already reported at publication time — 349 (morning Jul 12) and 342 (morning Jul 13) — setting a **hard floor of 349** that the max is virtually certain to meet or exceed. The real question is the maximum over the 11 remaining unknown nights.

**Method:** Monte Carlo mixture grounded in dense July 2026 empirical data (lull nights ~35%, active nights centered ~360, mass-wave nights ~10–13% centered ~540 with a 2026 record of 660). Over 11 nights there's roughly a 65–75% chance of at least one mass wave, placing the median around **520**.

**Distribution:** P10 ≈ 406, P50 ≈ 520, P90 ≈ 672, with ~12% chance of a new record (>660) and ~1% above 800 (open upper bound). Right-skewed, robust across mass-wave-frequency sensitivity scenarios (median 480–590). The premortem reviewer independently confirmed the estimate.
